sections.content: A modern marine VHF radio has several essential controls. Understanding each control's function is critical before operating the radio at sea. The main panel includes a channel selector, volume control, squelch adjustment, push-to-talk (PTT) button, and power output switch.

rows: Selects the operating VHF channel (1–88 international)
Adjusts the audio output level of received signals
Filters out background noise when no signal is present
Hold to transmit, release to receive
Switches between high power (25W) and low power (1W)
Monitors Channel 16 while listening on another channel
Accesses Digital Selective Calling functions on Channel 70

sections.title: Pre-Departure Check
sections.content: Always test your radio before departing by making a radio check call on a working channel. Verify that you can transmit and receive clearly.

sections.prompt: What does the squelch control do?

options.text: Filters out background noise when no signal is present
Switches between simplex and duplex mode

sections.explanation: The squelch control sets a threshold that silences the receiver when no signal is present, filtering out background static and noise.
